key_insight: The paper accelerates phase-field cohesive zone modeling to predict mixed-mode fracture and high-cycle fatigue of UHPC and HSS with reduced cycle-by-cycle simulation cost.
engineering_relevance: This enables more practical durability assessment of high-performance infrastructure materials under repeated loading, supporting faster design and life prediction workflows.
method: Derives a generic stress-based failure criterion for UHPC from biaxial tensile tests.
Adds a fatigue degradation law to the PF-CZM framework.
Uses an envelope-load acceleration scheme with adaptive cyclic increments to capture the three-stage fatigue process efficiently.
evidence_signal: The model is validated against relevant experimental comparisons and shown to reproduce mixed-mode fracture and high-cycle fatigue behavior of UHPC and HSS.
watch_item: The accelerated cycle approximation may depend on calibration and may be less reliable outside the loading regimes and material conditions covered by the validation data.
paper_title: High performance construction materials fracture and high cycle fatigue assessment based on accelerated PF-CZM
